text:
Market today: Jobs print came in soft - only 57K payrolls added, unemployment at 4.2%, labor force participation at a 50-year low outside Covid. That's a cooling economy signal. For tech/chips, soft macro cuts both ways: rate-cut hopes are mildly supportive, but if growth fears dominate, high-beta names get hit hardest. Tesla sinking on a rough day despite good deliveries suggests sentiment is fragile.

Your holdings: CRWD is the emergency - you're down 73% and your stop is $590 on a stock trading at $193. That stop is completely wrong, probably a data error. Needs immediate attention. SOXL is down 32.5% - it's a 3X leveraged ETF that decays, and on margin that's a compounding problem. Your stop at $200 is already blown. SPAL is down 14.4% with a stop at $21.38 - price is $21.53, you're one bad tick away from being stopped out.

Watch: CRWD stop level needs fixing now. Jobs data reaction in SMH - chips are your biggest sector bet.

Bottom line: Fix that CRWD stop and mind your leveraged positions before adding anything new.
plays: CRWD stop at $590 is clearly a data error - verify and reset immediately; price is $193.98 and you're already down 73%, wrong stop means no protection.
SOXL: stop at $200 is already breached, price $171.75, down 32.5% on a decaying 3X ETF on margin - decide today whether to cut or set a real new stop below current price.

No new buys today - macro is shaky, your leveraged positions need triage first; scanner shows CRWD long setup at entry $193.84/stop $192.53 but adding to a -73% position in current conditions is aggressive.
